Making an Impact Since 2010

Fueling Creative Economies Across America

NACED brings together artists, entrepreneurs, policymakers, and community leaders to build thriving local economies powered by creativity and culture.

Explore Our Programs Our Impact Story
1,200+
Artists & Makers Supported
48
Communities Served

Economic Impact Generated
15 yrs
Advancing Creative Economies
Our Mission

We believe creativity is an economic engine, not just an amenity.

For fifteen years, NACED has been at the intersection of arts and economics — helping communities recognize, develop, and sustain the creative assets that make them unique and economically resilient.

From small-town cultural districts to urban maker hubs, we provide the research, training, and funding that turns creative potential into lasting prosperity.

Learn about our story

"NACED's accelerator gave me the language and tools to talk to investors and city officials about what I was already doing intuitively. It changed everything."

T
Tanya Williams
Ceramic artist & studio owner, Detroit
What We Do

Our Programs

View all programs →
🚀

Creative Entrepreneur Accelerator

Business Skills for Artists

A 12-week intensive program equipping artists and makers with the business skills to turn their creative practice into a sustainable livelihood.

Learn more
🏘️

Community Cultural Planning

Policy Meets Place

We partner with cities and towns to develop cultural master plans that recognize and amplify local creative assets.

Learn more
🎭

Creative Placemaking Grants

Art That Transforms Space

Seed funding for public art, cultural districts, and creative reuse projects that transform underutilized spaces into community anchors.

Learn more

From the Field

News & Stories

All news →
Report March 9, 2026

How Creative Districts Are Revitalizing Rust Belt Cities

Five years into our partnership with three Midwestern cities, the data is clear: investing in arts infrastructure pays dividends far beyond the cultural sector.

By Maria Chen Read more →
Story February 24, 2026

Meet Jamal: From Food Truck to Brick-and-Mortar

Through NACED's Accelerator program, chef and entrepreneur Jamal Washington built the business acumen to open his first restaurant — and he's not stopping there.

By Lisa Park Read more →
Announcement January 31, 2026

NACED Releases 2025 State of Creative Economies Report

Our flagship annual report documents the health of creative economies across 48 communities, revealing both encouraging trends and persistent challenges.

By NACED Staff Read more →
Make an Impact

Your Gift Transforms Communities

Every dollar you give goes directly to programs that empower creative entrepreneurs, fund public art, and help cities build economies where creativity thrives.
